item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak traced to lettuce from Mexico used by Taco Bell
item.description: A lettuce supplier to fast-food giant Taco Bell has been linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak that has sickened thousands of people, the CDC said.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Fact checking Trump's new claims of election interference
item.description: In a primetime address on Thursday evening, President Trump accused China of interfering in U.S. elections as he questioned the integrity of the country's voting system. CBS News' Jake Rosen fact-checks his claims.

item.title: Over 80,000 fans and Trump expected at World Cup final between Spain and Argentina
item.description: Tens of thousands of fans, along with President Trump, are expected to gather in New Jersey on Sunday as Spain and Argentina battle for the World Cup trophy. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Colorado ICE center employee arrested after allegedly shooting protester
item.description: An employee of a group that operates an ICE detainment center in Aurora, Colorado, was arrested after police said he shot and wounded a woman who was taking part in a protest.

item.title: 7-foot-3 former pro basketball player sworn in as Texas police officer
item.description: At 7-foot-3, Jordan Wilmore was told his entire life that he was destined for a career in basketball. But that was never his dream. Steve Hartman has the story.

item.title: Jimothy, an unusual raccoon with a possible spinal condition, embraced by a Seattle community
item.description: Jimothy the raccoon has a condition known as short-spine syndrome, but he has been embraced by a Seattle community and the internet alike.

item.title: Trump revisits disputed claims about elections but offers no new proof of fraud
item.description: In a primetime address, President Trump alleged the U.S. election system falls "catastrophically short," revisiting a topic that has drawn his attention for years — and making claims that election experts have heavily disputed.

item.title: House panel probing Epstein asked Leon Black about birthday book, their friendship
item.description: Billionaire Leon Black, who paid Epstein $158 million for tax advice, was subpoenaed for a second interview with the House Oversight Committee in September.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

item.title: FDA identifies potential culprit in cyclosporiasis outbreak as illness spreads nationwide
item.description: Authorities have traced the cyclosporiasis outbreak to Mexican-grown lettuce delivered to Taco Bell by Taylor Farms. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: DHS Secretary Mullin doubles down on Trump's claims of election tampering
item.description: President Trump had harsh words for China in his speech on Thursday night. After calling President Xi Jinping a friend at a summit in May, he accused the communist country of "sinister election meddling." Ed O'Keefe has more details.

item.title: Smoke from Canadian wildfires blankets major U.S. cities for third straight day
item.description: Millions of Americans across major cities continued to face dangerous air conditions on Friday as smoke from Canadian wildfires lingered. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Rivers rise in Texas Hill Country after heavy rain, rescue efforts ongoing
item.description: Rivers across Texas Hill Country were still rising on Friday, after the region got more than two feet of rain. The downpours that began Monday were finally winding down, but the danger is not over. Jason Allen reports and Eric Fisher has the forecast.

item.title: Could poor air quality derail the World Cup final?
item.description: Air quality warnings in the Northeast have prompted some internal safety discussions with FIFA ahead of Sunday's World Cup final in New Jersey. MLS analyst and former professional soccer player Sacha Kljestan joins to discuss.

item.title: Trump endorses Lindsey Graham's sister Darline for full Senate term
item.description: President Trump on Friday afternoon urged Darline Graham, the sister of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, to run in South Carolina for a full Senate term. Bloomberg TV Washington correspondent Tyler Kendall and NOTUS politics and policy reporter Oriana González join to discuss.

item.title: The health impacts of poor air as wildfire smoke set to linger
item.description: Wildfire smoke from Canada, causing poor air quality in key U.S. cities, including New York and Chicago, is set to linger over the weekend. Dr. Carlos Gould, assistant professor at UC San Diego, joins CBS News to discuss the health implications.

item.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak as Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce
item.description: Produce supplier Taylor Farms announced Friday it was voluntarily recalling iceberg lettuce after it was linked to a nationwide cyclosporiasis outbreak. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins with the latest.

item.title: Trump doubles down on stolen election claims, accuses China of meddling
item.description: President Trump renewed his claims of election fraud in a primetime address on Thursday night, setting the stage for a tense midterms season. Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Feeding Our Future fraud ring's alleged No. 2 appears in court after Somalia arrest
item.description: Forty-two-year-old Abdikerm Eidleh appeared in federal court in St. Paul on Friday, just weeks after he was arrested in Mogadishu.

item.title: Strong earthquake hits Mexico-Guatemala border, tsunami alert lifted
item.description: The U.S. Geological Survey reported that the earthquake had a magnitude of 7.3 with the epicenter 30 miles southwest of Aquiles Serdan, Mexico.

item.title: Argentina takes on Spain in 2026 World Cup men's final
item.description: After more than 100 matches played in three different countries over the span of five weeks, the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup will conclude this weekend. Lionel Messi will lead defending champion Argentina against Spain, which punched its ticket to the final after beating France. Lukas Weese, a staff writer at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Installation failures caused Reflecting Pool damage, Washington Post analysis finds
item.description: A Washington Post analysis published this week found what experts say is the real cause of the damage to the Reflecting Pool in the nation's capital, and it has nothing to do with vandalism. Jarrett Ley, one of the reporters behind the analysis, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: The status of wildfire smoke as it moves down eastern U.S.
item.description: More than 100 million people across the northern half of the U.S. are under air quality alerts because of widespread wildfires in Canada and Minnesota. Heavy rain and extreme heat are also expected. CBS News national investigative correspondent Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Former Arizona election official reacts to Trump's primetime speech
item.description: President Trump railed against China in his remarks about U.S. election security on Thursday night. Stephen Richer, a former election official in Maricopa County, Arizona, joins with his reaction.

item.title: Voter data Trump claimed China obtained is easy to get in most states
item.description: President Trump called China's acquisition of voter information "the largest compromise of election data in history." In 20 states, anyone can get this information with a simple public request.

item.title: U.S. launches 7th straight day of Iran strikes as Strait of Hormuz freezes up
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran escalate attacks 140 days into the war, an industry analyst says "nobody is willing to move" through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump claims China compromised voter data that's already publicly available
item.description: One of President Trump's biggest claims in his Thursday night election security speech was that China accessed more than 200 million Americans' voter information. But, as CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto explains, it's already publicly available.

item.title: 7/17: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump claims catastrophic election vulnerabilities without proof; Washington Post identifies installation failures as cause of Reflecting Pool damage.

item.title: Can you spot an AI image? Quiz shows how difficult identifying deepfakes has become.
item.description: An AI expert said the telltale signs people once relied on, such as distorted fingers, unnatural eyes, or other obvious visual flaws, have largely disappeared as technology has improved.
